examples.title: Basic ABAC Policy Evaluation
examples.description: Demonstrates a simple ABAC policy evaluation in Python with subject, resource, and environment attributes
examples.explanation: This Python code implements a basic ABAC policy engine that evaluates access requests against defined policies using subject, resource, action, and environment attributes. It demonstrates how multiple conditions are combined with AND logic to make access decisions.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the number of policies
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m is the number of stored policies

examples.title: ABAC Access Control Algorithm
examples.description: Pseudocode showing the complete ABAC access control flow from request to enforcement
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ABAC_Access_Control
BEGIN
    FUNCTION Handle_Access_Request(user, resource, action, context)
        // Step 1: PEP intercepts the access request
        request ← CREATE_Request(user, resource, action, context)
        
        // Step 2: Collect attributes from PIP
        subject_attrs ← PIP.Get_Subject_Attributes(user.id)
        resource_attrs ← PIP.Get_Resource_Attributes(resource.id)
        action_attrs ← CREATE_Action_Attributes(action)
        env_attrs ← PIP.Get_Environment_Attributes(context)
        
        // Step 3: Build complete request with attributes
        complete_request ← BUILD_Request(
            subject_attrs, 
            resource_attrs, 
            action_attrs, 
            env_attrs
        )
        
        // Step 4: Send to PDP for evaluation
        policies ← PAP.Get_All_Policies()
        decision ← PDP.Evaluate(complete_request, policies)
        
        // Step 5: Return decision to PEP
        IF decision = "PERMIT" THEN
            RETURN GRANT_Access(resource, action)
        ELSE
            RETURN DENY_Access(resource, action)
        END IF
    END FUNCTION
    
    // Example policy evaluation
    FUNCTION Evaluate_Policy(request, policy)
        FOR EACH condition IN policy.conditions
            attr_value ← GET_Attribute_Value(request, condition.attribute_name)
            IF NOT COMPARE(attr_value, condition.operator, condition.value) THEN
                RETURN "DENY"
            END IF
        END FOR
        RETURN "PERMIT"
    END FUNCTION
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode illustrates the complete ABAC access control flow: PEP intercepts the request, PIP retrieves attributes, PDP evaluates against policies from PAP, and PEP enforces the final decision.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n×m) where n=policies, m=conditions per policy

examples.title: ABAC Policy Decision Point (PDP) Implementation
examples.description: Java implementation showing how a PDP evaluates access requests against ABAC policies
examples.explanation: This Java code implements a Policy Decision Point (PDP) that evaluates incoming access requests against multiple policies. It demonstrates attribute retrieval from different categories and comparative evaluation using various operators.
examples.timeComplexity: O(p × c) where p=number of policies, c=conditions per policy
examples.spaceComplexity: O(s + r + a + e) where s/r/a/e = subject/resource/action/environment attributes
